title: The Sonnets LXIV - When I have seen by Timeï؟½s fell hand defacï؟½d
content:
آ آ آ آ When I have seen by Timeï؟½s fell hand defacï؟½d
آ آ آ آ The rich-proud cost of outworn buried age;
آ آ آ آ When sometime lofty towers I see down-razï؟½d,
آ آ آ آ And brass eternal slave to mortal rage;
آ آ آ آ When I have seen the hungry ocean gain
آ آ آ آ Advantage on the kingdom of the shore,
آ آ آ آ And the firm soil win of the watery main,
آ آ آ آ Increasing store with loss, and loss with store;
آ آ آ آ When I have seen such interchange of state,
آ آ آ آ Or state itself confounded, to decay;
آ آ آ آ Ruin hath taught me thus to ruminate 
آ آ آ آ That Time will come and take my love away.
آ آ آ آ This thought is as a death which cannot choose
آ آ آ آ But weep to have, that which it fears to lose.
meaning:
This evocative piece by William Shakespeare, titled "The Sonnets LXIV - When I have seen by Timeï؟½s fell hand defacï؟½d", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
